answer:

                                                                                   ÔÉľ    2
        labels with many products.
        Answers relating to the need for providing information about the product in

        Guidance:
         ‚ÄĘ Look for detailed understanding.
         ‚ÄĘ Energy labels allow consumers to compare products prior to
            purchasing.
         ‚ÄĘ Decisions may be made on an efficiency basis, consumers should
            ethically / morally be made aware of the running costs of products prior
            to purchase.
         ‚ÄĘ Directive 2010/30/EU ‚Äúon the indication by labelling and standard
            product information of the consumption of energy and other resources
            by energy-related products‚ÄĚ sets a framework for the adoption of
            product-specific directives on the proper energy efficiency labelling for
            each concerned product.
         ‚ÄĘ This law is undergoing review; the proposed legislation, simplifying
            labelling categories and extending the scope will likely be adopted in
            2017.
         ‚ÄĘ Suppliers are to supply free of charge labels or product fiches
            containing information about consumption of electric or other energy
            sources to their dealers. Dealers display labels in a visible and legible
            way, and make the fiche available in product brochures or other
            literature.

        Simplistic or basic response                                                    1
        Energy labels are needed to give customers information
        Or
        It is a mandatory requirement to have a label                                   1
        More detailed explanation or response
        Manufacturers are required to provide energy data with new products so that     2
        customers / purchasers can clearly see the energy consumption of products.

         (ii) Explain in detail the benefits of energy labels to the
                                                                                    ÔÉľ   5
        consumer when purchasing products.
        Answers relating to benefits to the consumer of energy labels should be
        awarded up to 5 marks.

        Guidance:
         ‚ÄĘ Consumers can clearly see the energy rating of the product.
         ‚ÄĘ Labels simplify running costs, the higher the A rating the more
            economical / efficient the product is.
         ‚ÄĘ Consumers can compare products using the labelling system.
         ‚ÄĘ Consumers are made fully aware of running costs of the product.
         ‚ÄĘ Consumers can make more informed judgements based on eco factors.
         ‚ÄĘ Consumers can also be informed of noise generation of products.
         ‚ÄĘ Consumers can judge products on rrp and runnings costs ‚Äď a cheaper

        Brief or basic explanation, very little detail.

        Consumer can see how much energy the product will use

        consumer

        Consumer can see how much energy will be used and can compare to
        another product.
        More detailed explanation with a clear understanding of the impact on
        consumer.
        Consumer can see how much energy is likely to be used and can estimate          3
        running costs / bills effectively. The customer can compare similar products

        A detailed explanation with a clear understanding of impact on the
        consumer.

        bills effectively. The consumer can compare similar products and consider

        A highly detailed and comprehensive explanation of both positive and
